mission.title: dapr: Enable GET for secret stores to dynamically fetch secrets
mission.description: Enable GET for secret stores to dynamically fetch secrets. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current dapr deployment
steps.description:
Verify your dapr version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
helm list -n dapr 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working dapr installation.

steps.title: Review dapr configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant dapr configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
kubectl get configmap -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=dapr
```
## Current situation

Dapr already supports various [secret stores](https://github.com/dapr/components-contrib/blob/master/secretstores/Readme.md) for [referencing secrets inside Components spec.metadata

steps.title: Apply the fix for Enable GET for secret stores to dynamically fetch secrets
steps.description:
Enabling GET for secret stores to dynamically fetch secrets as described in #1034. Current work just enables the http endpoint.
```yaml
#### URL Parameters

Parameter | Description
--------- | -----------
daprPort | the Dapr port
secretstorename |
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
kubectl get events -n dapr --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Enable GET for secret stores to dynamically fetch secrets" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Enabling GET for secret stores to dynamically fetch secrets as described in #1034. Current work just enables the http endpoint.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with dapr installed or the issue environment reproducible.
